sentences.text: Hey, how are you, Stella?
Hey, I'm doing good, Helen.
Good, I'm doing well.
Doing well.
You're down in Mobile, right?
bootyp signal.
Okay.
Have you always lived down there?
I'm sorry, I'm sorry.
Sorry, what was that?
I said, have you… have you always lived down in Mobile?
No, I'm actually from, like, North Alabama, but I've been to Mobile, I moved here for college, and I graduated, like.
Okay.
So I've been here ever since then.
You said how long ago?
A year and a half ago.
Oh, a year and a half ago.
Apologies.
Nice!
Where… where did you go?
South.
Okay.
University of South Florida right now?
I, … I came here, like, 2 weeks after I graduated, and I've been here ever since.
Oh, nice.
Did you go down there just to be closer to the beach, or…?
Yeah, I have something here, so….
Okay.
I've got a friend that's from Mobile, and he lives….
Thank you.
In, … what's the city, like, literally right north of Mobile?
I'm gonna have to look it up.
Popular posting, this home.
Don't support it.
It's a difference.
Let's see here… He lives in….
Thank you.
Oh, I cannot find it.
I don't know anymore.
Is it, like, bear out versus English report or something like that?
Oh, no, it's, Foley.
Foley.
Holy, okay, yeah.
There we go.
But him and his wife live down there now, but he's from Mobile, and used to work on the beach, and I was always jealous, because I'm up here in Tennessee, so there is no beach in Tennessee.
Yeah, okay.
So you're from North Alabama, though, like the Huntsville area, or…?
Yeah, I'm, like, an hour from Huntsville.
What's the name of the city?
It's called Muscle Shoulders.
Okay, I've heard of that.
I have… I should have came more prepared with the names of the cities in Alabama.
I have family that lives up by Huntsville.
Yeah.
That's one thing that I'll reach out for.
But it's, like, an hour outside of Huntsville.
We'll search design.
No, I'm sorry, Madison.
Madison's not that far.
I was just about to say, it's probably Mark, so….
Madison, and then my grandfather lived in Fayetteville, Tennessee, so he would, like, Hazel.
claims.
It's like, an every-weekend type of adventure.
Whoever?
Back when he was a cigar.
And then this class.
It's pretty familiar.
I'm somewhat… I wouldn't say pretty familiar, I'm somewhat familiar with it.
I'm so worried.
Yeah, I did some travel sales, too, so, like, I was down in Huntsville a couple of times.
But it's surface that.
It's just, it's been so long that I feel like….
everything's just changed.
Huntsville is tremendously… Hazel Green is nothing like what it used to be.
Yeah.
So… Well, cool.
Well, thank you for hopping on with me today.
Yeah, so Abby is actually tied up right now, but she… I kind of just handle all of the marketing, so I feel like I would just be relaying it to her anyways.
Okay.
But yeah, she'd gotten the email, and, we had talked about it, and it seemed a little interesting.
We are in the process of, like.

obviously integrating a lot more AI and things like that, we're getting… like, we've been doing all of our applications, and we're now getting this… It's called this software that basically does it for us.
Yeah.
And so I'm trying to integrate more things that are automated workflows, like.
Chatbots that can kind of weed things out, leads out, and answer questions that we answer every day, because our phone just rings off.
So, … and our office is really small, there's, like.
I think 15 of us, and that includes… Well, I mean, not 15, maybe, like, 20 without maintenance.
But our leasing team's super small, and … We have… Over 30 properties, over 800 units, so we're… we're big, but we do it on a small… with a small team, so… Yeah, it seems.
Trying to take some workload off of your plates and make your lives a little easier, I get it.
It's… it was… it was crazy for us, … Yeah, I mean, it's kind of slowing down now, but it was… it was hectic for a while.
I believe it, everybody… it's like, a lot of… Do a lot of people move to Mobile?
I feel like they would if they're, like, in Alabama.
I feel like they do, and, like, I knew… I knew tons of people in college that were coming from, like, a lot of people from Chicago would come here.

It's like, it's very random, and then obviously we have stuff in, … like Orange Beach, and Fairloak, and things like that, so people want to be pushed to the beach, … And we're… we're licensed in Florida, Georgia, Alabama, Louisiana, … Mississippi, so we have properties pretty much everywhere.
So… Yeah, it's… And we're constantly taking on new properties.
Seth?
Sounds like a lot.
Yes.
So, does… does… I guess you're a part of Toomey Properties, correct?
Is that… does that act separate as, like, the other entities under SVN?
So, we're independently owned, but SBN is national brand, so, but yes.
Gotcha, okay, that's fine.

I, I… so, like, when I, like, look in our system, we have SVN, and then there is a million and seven contacts, and I'm like… but they're also saying, like, when I look, you guys, like, when I've looked up Abigail, it was like, but Toomey Properties, and I'm like, well.
Yeah.
And then you go onto SVN's website, and there's, like, 600 properties, so I'm like, what's going on?
Okay, so you guys act independent, or you guys are independently owned, you basically act as your own entity, it sounds like, under the SVN brand.
Yes, it's… it's basically just, like, just having the backing of a national brand, and having those connections and things like that.
Like, we, we don't do a ton of, like, commercial real estate, but, it's mostly just property management, which… Most of the other SBM offices are not really a lot of property management.
Yeah.
… But that's something that just kind of went to the road for us.
I don't remember what I was gonna say, but yeah, SV is huge.
It's like… and every time I get on some kind of platform, they'll, like.
be like, hey, SVN International, and I'm like.
It's us!
But I guess it just comes up that way.
Yeah, yeah.
Okay.
Well, that's fine.
With… with what you guys do now, I know you said you're integrating a lot of softwares.
What property management software are you using?
the old one.
Buildium, okay.
Unfortunately.
I've heard that name a handful of times, it's like a lot of people are starting to wean it out.
Which I guess is a good thing.
Yeah.
We're… I mean, we're so used to it this… at this point, but I… I'm not a fan of Buildium, but… I mean, that's what they've always used, so….
I'm trying to look them up, because I've heard that name, and I feel like we integrate with them.
But I don't want to tell you… I'm sorry, I'm… I'm… you're in front of me, but I'm also looking at this other thing.
Amazing.
Buildium… do they have a different name, too?
Like, is Buildium a part of… I feel like Bildium's a part of, like, Resman, aren't they?
Oh, I… I thought that they were their own thing.
They may be, I don't know anymore.
Regardless, we can make it work.
Whether we integrate with them or we have a manual build, we can advertise properties, so that's not a big deal.
CRM.
Are you using them for a CRM as well, or do you have something separate, or is it just kind of….
Dun
Yeah, building is… it's, it's all that we use.
We use it for pretty much everything, other than… We don't really use, … it for the leads or anything like that.
… Yeah.
Those all go to our… release an email, and we just filter through those, because I know that building has, like, a… Prospects tab.
Yeah.
And, before, I've, like, tried to, get on other softwares, and they'll put it in that tab, and we don't check that ever.
Gotcha.
Nonsense.
And I… so I looked them up.
They're actually… they're owned by RealPage, which, that's the name that was going through my head.
But we… we… so, if that's the case, I'm 99.
5% sure we integrate with them.
So, when saying that, that means, like, when you're updating, like, availability and stuff on your end, it'll pull and it'll reflect on our end.
Gotcha.
Without having to go in and manually update things.
Perfect.
Cool.
So, when it comes to advertising right now, what are you currently doing for it?
All of our properties are really different, just depending on, obviously, like, occupancy and what the owners are willing to do.
Mainly, it's Apartments.
com and Zillow.
I know we have one that uses, apartment list.
And… we also have, like, a good bit of Section 8, so that's, like, affordable housing.
Okay.
But that's pretty much it.
We do… we do a little bit of, like, Facebook, and I like to do occasional, like, social media ads.
But that's usually, like, a last resort kind of thing.
And you handle that yourself.
Yes.
Gotcha.
And you said roughly 30 properties, most are Section 8, and then… ….
Yeah, I would say… Yeah, I guess most are Section 8.
I guess… to… when I think about it, it's kind of like a half and half, but I guess, yeah, most jurisdiction.
We have, like, You know, Class A properties and Class C properties, it's all over.
Okay.
plus A to C class.
Cool.
Perfect.
So you have a mixture of Section 8A and conventional.
I'm assuming Section 8's probably pretty filled up, and then how's conventional side doing?
It all just depends, but yeah, section 8 is usually….
a lot easier.
Yeah.
Easier in the leasing aspect, not.
Right.
And the other side of things, but yeah.
Right.
Yeah.
Okay, so, but occupancy for the conventional, it's like here and there, depending on the property, and….
Yeah, like, you know, sometimes we'll get lease-ups for either one, so, … Yeah, just kinda… kinda varies.
Okay.
That works.
So… so your main focus is trying to take some workload off your plate, find something that's a smooth, easy process.
AI is always a good thing.
Like what you mentioned, so that's what… kind of what you're looking for?
Are you looking for a way to drive more renter traffic to you as well?

Yeah, definitely, and I was… I was curious about, like, what y'all's audience is, and how wide it is, because I've… I've never heard of y'all before, and so I didn't know, like, if it's just the one site, or if you have multiple, … But yeah, and then also being… Weeding out, you know, … Leads that are serious, and ones that aren't, and having those conversations that we have with tons of people every day, just, like, answering those simple questions.
Yeah.
Because if we get a lead, it just….
like, say, Apartments.

com, and it just gives us their contact, and it's just like, there you go, and then we'll call them up, and then it's either, you know, we have to call them, and they may not be looking to move right now.
… you know, they may not even have looked at the listing really well, and they don't even know what they're looking at.
So, yeah, that part's definitely interesting to me.
Okay.
And I believe that's it with the, with, that software, but….
Okay.
I thought there was something else to that, because I was trying to do some research and, see what, what all y'all did.
Like, I feel like there's something else that I'm missing.
We, … We offer a lot.
I'll dive in and kind of show you everything, too.
Okay.

So that way you get a full grasp, but in terms of driving more traffic your way, making sure you're getting more of a quality renter versus just wasting your time with that person that's moving in 6 months.
We can definitely help out with those aspects.

If you're using Buildium and RowPage, we integrate with Ropage, so when we deliver leads, we can deliver it directly into a CRM, but we can make them very specified, … And ask for a lot of details, so, like, if it is that person that's moving in 6 months, you don't have to call them right now.
Correct.
We used to have a chatbot, feature for, like, our social component.
… We kind of sunset that, just because it was… It wasn't the best.
people would respond to it or not respond to it, and if they didn't respond to it, then companies were like, how do we get in contact with this person still?
And so we just, like, kind of eliminated that to allow the teams the time to be able to.
To communicate with them personally and kind of figure out exactly what they're needing in a split second.
Yeah.
But yeah, I think there's some things that we could do to help you out, and… At least make it to where you're not calling, like, unqualified leads.
Right, yeah.
So….
We just… I'm just trying to… I don't know if you're familiar with apartment list at all.
But, ….
They do use that trap bod, and we just started it for, like, one of our properties, because one of our owners was like, we have it on this.
I was like, I've never heard of it.
And they use that chatbot feature, and… I'm like, I'm just very curious about it, because, like you said, like.
When it, … when they start talking to them, and they don't get a response, and it's like, how do we get back in touch with them?
Yeah.

And I also worry about… I mean, obviously, like, AI is taking over, but I also worry about them not having… Their answers, or their questions answered right, or, like, not having… I feel like a lot of people just like to get that call from us, and just, like, hear that we're… Because people will call all the time and be like, I'm all a scam?
And I'm like, no.
So y'all don't have that feature.
We don't have that feature.
I can suggest you a couple that are really good.
I haven't heard too much on apartment lists, but I think if… I think they used the same technology, in a sense, that we were using for ours.
Yeah.
And the one that I would suggest to you is… Probably one of the biggest ones, and it helps schedule tours as well.
Yeah.
It's called Elise AI.
Okay.
It's a RealPage product as well, so since you're already using Buildium, like, it may be something that they can just kind of work in with you, since it's one of their products.

But Elise, like the person, E-L-I-S-E, AI, it's super good, we integrate with it as well, so when we deliver leads, we deliver it to that, and then they can pick up on the conversation, schedule the tour, whatever you need done.
In terms of what we offer, I'll share my screen here.
So… We're an internet listing site.
Very similar to, like, a Zillow, ApartmentList, and Apartments.
com.
We differ in a lot of ways.
One way in particular is because we have such a wide cast net compared to those providers, and then the other one is how cost-effective we are.
We're pennies to the dollar compared to your Apartments.
com or a Zillow spend.
Hmm.
When it comes to what we offer.
we typically integrate with your property management software.
Since you're using Buildium, I'm pretty sure we integrate with them, so anytime you're updating availability, pricing, pictures on your end, it would pull and reflect on ours.
From there, we would advertise your properties on our rentable site in itself, and then from there, we would syndicate it out.
And when we syndicate out, we go everywhere except for Apartments.
com.
So, what we do, though, is we have an AI tool working in the background to make sure we're not duplicating any listings.
So, it's gonna make sure, like, for your property, since you're using Zillow, basically, portfolio-wide, we wouldn't push and duplicate a property there.

If you have one property on apartment list, for an example, we wouldn't push and duplicate it there, but then we would fill in the gaps for your other 29 properties and still get you to the apartment list for those.
So it's really just making sure that we're not duplicating anything.
And then, from there, we have an all-inclusive targeted social ad piece.
I know you mentioned you do some of those in-house.
We set it up to where they're doable on every single property at no additional cost, and we're essentially reaching out to those renters for you versus waiting for them to come to you.
Right.
So, the way it'll work is anytime somebody goes on Google and says, show me an apartment in… show me apartments in Mobile.
Next time they get on Facebook or Instagram, we'll hit them with a targeted rentable ad that looks like this.
It'll have pictures of your individual properties, or your individual property, sorry, one ad, one property.
It'll have details about it, if it's pet-friendly, if there's any, you know, a washer-dryer, if it allows pets, whatever it may be.
And then where we differ on our social ads from a lot of other companies that could do these for you.
Those other companies usually do one of two things.
They either redirect them to their personal site, and then that renter can see every single person and company that they're advertising for.
And then the other way they usually do is they redirect them to a property website, which, at that point you lose track of the renter.
Did they become a lead?
Did they not?
You don't really know.
Hi.

All these are lead generating for you, so we capture their names, their emails, their phone numbers, and then we can deliver them to you as a guest card into your CRM, or into an on-site leasing email address, or both, whichever you prefer.
And then, in terms of, like, the quality issue, you said you don't want to waste your time, like, calling a lot of people, because it's always ringing off the hook.
With these, we can ask a ton of questions to make sure that that person is a serious renter, and it asks majority… or answers majority of their questions, in a sense.
When are you looking to move?
What floor plan are you looking for?
… Are you bringing pets?
Right?
Whatever it may be, we can ask, like, a lot of those questions to kind of eliminate the need for you having to ask those on your end.
And then… yeah.
generate.
And, … I hate social media ads, like, I hate creating them.

I think they're great, but I hate, like… Instagram and Facebook, it's just the whole… The way that, like, Meta has their ad manager and, like, everything… I hate all of that, it's terrible, I hate doing it.
What's happening?
It used to be easy.
I mean, I guess it's easy, and I….
It used to be.
Now it's difficult.
It's like, I can't even find the Ad Manager tab anymore.
There's so many tabs, and there's so many, like, I'll go in, my CFO will be like, can you send me a receipt?
And I go in, and I'm like, I have to go all over in this specific I'm just not a fan of it.
… So that's… yeah, that would be awesome.
Does it, like… Is it always live, or does it kind of….
Yeah, so it's basically….
Or does that get just depending on… How much we're paying, or…?
Yeah, so it's basically like a running ad.
It doesn't just last for, like, an hour or two, and then it, like, refreshes every, like, every other day.
It refreshes, like, 3 times a week, so….
Okay.
But yeah, we run it all month long.
And we run it every month.
So, but we're using AI when it generates these, so, like.
this month it'll be one, or, like, this week it'll be one ad, and the next week it could be another.
And the AI tool's learning, okay, which picture… in terms of, like, the order of things that things are put, if the pool is showing before the kitchen.
which one of those is getting more interaction and engagement?
If it's the little kitchen, then it'll move the kitchen to the front more often.
Gotcha.
So it kind of learns itself as well.
Then….
And it takes all the pain out of having to go in and post them manually.
Yeah, I love when… and then not only post it manually, but have to monitor it and see, like, which listing is performing better, or which… social media ad or whatever.
Yeah, it's… I love when AI does my job for me.
That's the great thing about AI.
The scary part is we don't know how advanced it's gonna be in the future.
Yeah.
So, pricing-wise, we do on a per-property basis.
Our price is, like I said, pennies to the dollar to anapartments.
com, though.
I also forgot to mention, the same AI tool that we have, making sure we're not duplicating any listings, is making sure we're getting you on the sites that perform the best as well.

So, like, if we're syndicating to 30 different sites, it'll say, here's your top 3 and here's your bottom 3, so instead of us trying to get results out of these bottom 3, we can put that spin towards the top 3 and double down on the results.
Okay.
So, making sure we're getting you in the right places.
But our price, it's only $199 a month per property.
Okay.
And they get you everything.
We typically require a 5-property minimum when somebody starts.
But it gets you our rentable site, our partnerships, our Facebook social ads, access to our backend dashboard, our AI tools, everything.
So it doesn't matter the size of the property, or…?
It could be a 400-unit complex, or it could be a 100-unit complex.
It's still 100.
That's interesting.
What's your typical property size?
So our biggest we have is… we have one in Pensacola, Florida, and it's 210 units.
And then, we have, like, obviously… a good bit of single-family homes.
I think… We have quite a few, … I mean, they're… they're… they really range.
Like, I think our smallest is… I think it's, like, 12 units, or something like that?
… Okay.
They're, like, all over the place.
How… how many properties do you have in total?
I wanna say… 30?
Okay.
I don't know if it's currently….
That's a mixture of the multi-family and some single-family?
Yes.
Okay.
I can't keep up with it, ever.
I may have a better pricing solution for you, then.
Okay.
And the reason I say that is because we do work with a bunch of scattered portfolios, right?
Like, where it could be a duplex, it could also be a 218-unit complex.
It's more of, like, our unlimited pricing, and what we do different with it is we don't give you a social boost for every single one of the properties.
But what it does is it allows us to advertise all 30 of your properties.
And then we can give you 3 rotatable social boosts with it that you can rotate every month to different properties.
So, like, let's say this month you put it on the 218-unit property, but next month you say you need a lot more traffic towards your 50-unit property.
You can move one of them to that.
So, it's like, it's still getting you monthly exposure at 3 different properties, but it's not getting at all the properties at all… every month.
Does that make sense?
Yeah, so when… so when, like, the 3 is up, that's… that's it, it's up.
No, no, no, no, no, no, sorry.
3 different social ads, like, for 3 different properties.
So, like, when we run social ads, we're running it for 3 different properties at a time, versus all 30 of your properties.
So, it'll run all month.
the same way that it would if I did one on every single one of your properties, like, it's included in this $199 a month price.
Gotcha.
But the overall price for the unlimited package is $9.
99, and that gets you all 30 of your properties on our platform, and then it gets you 3 rotatable social boosts.
So, 1 boost all month could run on your 218-unit complex, another one could run on another complex, this one can run on another.
And what I mean by rotatable is, next month.
Let's say you don't need it on this smaller property over here, because it's fully leased, or it doesn't need as much traffic as Property number 4 over here.
So you can kind of move those around.
It's not just, like, 3 ads, and that's it.
It's still the same process of it refreshes every other, like, every other day, it refreshes 3 times a week, and then it keeps running.
Okay.
It's just instead of having 30 properties and 30 ads, it's all 30 properties and only 3 ads that you can move amongst your properties.
Gotcha.
Hopefully I didn't confuse you there.
No, that made sense.
Okay.
when it comes to, like, bringing them all on at once, I really doubt that we'd be able to do that, just because… All of our owners.
We have tons of different owners.
And they all have different appetites for marketing, and, I mean, it doesn't matter sometimes what the property, you know, is a lease up or not.
Some of them are just not willing to do things, and some are.
I feel like it might get… a little confusing, but I mean….
Gotcha.
So, all your properties are third-party managed?
Sorry, they're what?
Are they… so you guys third-party manage all of your properties?
Yes, so we… we only own a few of them, but for the most part, we just manage them, and I mean.

Like, we… I just have to run things by the owners, and they either say yes or no, and if they say no, I just have to… So I can't… I don't think we'd be able to bring them all on, and I also put you… like, if we were to, you know, like, bring someone, I would make them all… aware of the benefits, and then some would probably choose, and then some wouldn't.
… Gotcha.
But I think for… It would probably just be, like, a slow process, because, like, we're always getting… new properties, and… A lot of times, when I get…
when I talk to the owners, and they're like, hey, what would pricing look like for… I'm kind of rambling, sorry, but what would pricing look like for, like, Zillow or Apartments.
com?
And I'll send it over to them, and it's, like, two grand a month, and they're like, okay, no, I'm not doing any of that.
So I could just….
send them this, and I'll be like, we can start with this several weeks slowly.
Build it, but probably not, like, all at once.
Yeah.
No, and that's fine.
That was just, I guess, a different option for you, because I do understand having 12 units, it's harder to fit in a 199 budget than it is for 218.
And, like, I guess the way a lot of our partners that do the unlimited deal is they'll kind of pitch it to the owner, like, hey, like.
you know, Zillow, like you said, is $2K a month, and if you divide that $9.
99 amongst 30 properties, it breaks down to, like.
basically $30 a property.
A little more than, like, $32 a property or something.
So it's, like, an easier pitch to… to owners like that.
But if you just need to start with, like, a per-property basis, and then down the road we can look at moving to an unlimited deal, we can look at that, too.
Okay.
So… Do you have an idea of properties that you'd want to bring on if you moved forward?
So… Obviously, I'm thinking the first one that comes to mind is the 218-minute property.
They are in the process of selling it, and we're kind of, like.
I mean, it's, like, 50% vacant at this point, because it was once, like, a terrible property, and they kind of revamped it, so, they've been trying every trick in the book to get that leased out.
Thank you.
It's basically in lease-up phase at this point.
And it's been for a long time.
severe… pulling out all the soaps at this point.
I'm also thinking of, …
We have another owner who has… What's just that?
I know there's one in Georgia, it's 52 units.
And then there's another in Mobile.

And that one is… I'm definitely thinking our bigger… Like, the comedies on the… Yeah, that one's 103 units, the automobile, so, like… Thinking the larger ones, … are… Our luxury… we have some, like, luxury properties, like.
the ones towards the beach and stuff like that.
I probably wouldn't do that for this unless, like, we got one that was, like, a lease of.
And maybe just like any other properties that we have at the moment that we just can't… Can't lease, which I can think of a few.
Yeah, and I can also do something, too, like, if it's easier, where I just make it where it's, like, 5 rotatable properties, too.
So, like, in your case, you have those 3 that you know that are probably the ones for sure, but then you have smaller unit ones.
So, you have one that… maybe you have… do you have an ownership group that has, like, multiple properties?
So, like, maybe they have, like, a 12-unit complex, but then… and it's on there, but let's say you lease all those 12 units up.
You don't want to just keep spending the $199, so you could move that to a different property for them, or for anybody, and put it on there.
I can do something like that, too.
Yeah, that would be… that would be the best, I think, because… Like, we have one owner.
And he has 4 properties with us.
And they're… they're really nice, and so… I know one of them is, like, fully leased.
And then there's another that's… the vacancy's constantly coming up because people are moving out.
Right?
So I think… a thing like that would be… Would be nice to have, for sure.
Okay.
A good bit of our owners, like, have several properties with us, but we do have some that just have one.
Okay.
So it's a… it's a mix there, too.
Yeah, everything with us is like a mixed bag.
